reasoning: The Lean formalization faithfully captures the informal statement. (1) `[Finite A]` encodes "finite set A". (2) `hSdistinct` encodes "S is a set of ordered triples of distinct elements". (3) The three conditions (hS1, hS2, hS3) properly quantify over triples/quadruples with pairwise distinct elements via `List.Pairwise (· ≠ ·)` — this matches the informal statement which scopes the conditions to "ordered triples of distinct elements"; the distinctness is needed for consistency (e.g., condition 2 with a=c would give a false iff). (4) The conclusion `∃ g : A → ℝ, Injective g ∧ ∀ a b c, g a < g b ∧ g b < g c → (a,b,c) ∈ S` precisely captures "there exists a one-to-one g with g(a)<g(b)<g(c) ⟹ (a,b,c) ∈ S" — note that injectivity of g combined with the strict inequalities automatically ensures a,b,c are distinct, so no extra distinctness hypothesis is needed there. No hypotheses are added or dropped; the quantifier structure matches.
